title: Visualise Relativistic Spacetime
description: Build interactive spacetime diagrams that illustrate time dilation, length contraction, simultaneity breakdown, and the twin paradox in special relativity.
whyItMatters: Special relativity defies everyday intuition. Spacetime diagrams make the abstract concrete — they show why moving clocks tick slowly, why simultaneity is relative, and why faster-than-light travel leads to paradoxes. Building these visualisations cements relativistic thinking.

steps.title: Minkowski diagram basics
steps.description: Plot a spacetime diagram with x (horizontal) and ct (vertical) axes. Draw the light cone (45-degree lines). Plot worldlines for stationary and moving observers.
steps.tips: Use ct rather than t on the vertical axis so light travels at 45 degrees.

steps.title: Lorentz transformation
steps.description: Implement the Lorentz transformation and show how the axes of a moving frame are tilted. Plot events in both frames.
steps.tips: The x' and ct' axes are symmetrically tilted toward the light cone.

steps.title: Time dilation visualisation
steps.description: Show a clock ticking in two frames. Mark events (ticks) on the worldline and demonstrate that the moving clock ticks more slowly in the stationary frame.
steps.tips: Use colour coding to distinguish frames and annotate the time intervals.

steps.title: Twin paradox
steps.description: Simulate the twin paradox: one twin stays home, the other travels at 0.8c to a star and returns. Show both worldlines and calculate the age difference.
steps.tips: The travelling twin's worldline has a kink (acceleration) — this breaks the symmetry.

steps.title: Interactive velocity slider
steps.description: Add a slider that lets the user change the relative velocity from 0 to 0.99c and see how the spacetime diagram transforms in real time.
steps.tips: Use matplotlib sliders or switch to Plotly/Streamlit for a smoother interactive experience.

deliverables: Minkowski diagrams for stationary and moving observers
Time dilation and twin paradox visualisations
Written explanation of each relativistic effect

rubric.description: Lorentz transformations correctly implemented, predictions match theory
Diagrams are clear, well-labelled, and pedagogically effective
Correctly shows asymmetry and age difference
Velocity slider works smoothly and updates in real time
